from lfx.base.data.utils import IMG_FILE_TYPES, TEXT_FILE_TYPES
from lfx.base.io.chat import ChatComponent
from lfx.inputs.inputs import BoolInput
from lfx.io import (
    DropdownInput,
    FileInput,
    MessageTextInput,
    MultilineInput,
    Output,
)
from lfx.schema.message import Message
from lfx.utils.constants import (
    MESSAGE_SENDER_AI,
    MESSAGE_SENDER_NAME_USER,
    MESSAGE_SENDER_USER,
)


class ChatInput(ChatComponent):
    display_name = "Chat Input"
    description = "Get chat inputs from the Playground."
    documentation: str = "https://docs.langflow.org/chat-input-and-output"
    icon = "MessagesSquare"
    name = "ChatInput"
    minimized = True

    inputs = [
        MultilineInput(
            name="input_value",
            display_name="Input Text",
            value="",
            info="Message to be passed as input.",
            input_types=[],
        ),
        BoolInput(
            name="should_store_message",
            display_name="Store Messages",
            info="Store the message in the history.",
            value=True,
            advanced=True,
        ),
        DropdownInput(
            name="sender",
            display_name="Sender Type",
            options=[MESSAGE_SENDER_AI, MESSAGE_SENDER_USER],
            value=MESSAGE_SENDER_USER,
            info="Type of sender.",
            advanced=True,
        ),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="sender_name",
            display_name="Sender Name",
            info="Name of the sender.",
            value=MESSAGE_SENDER_NAME_USER,
            advanced=True,
        ),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="session_id",
            display_name="Session ID",
            info="The session ID of the chat. If empty, the current session ID parameter will be used.",
            advanced=True,
        ),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="context_id",
            display_name="Context ID",
            info="The context ID of the chat. Adds an extra layer to the local memory.",
            value="",
            advanced=True,
        ),
        FileInput(
            name="files",
            display_name="Files",
            file_types=TEXT_FILE_TYPES + IMG_FILE_TYPES,
            info="Files to be sent with the message.",
            advanced=True,
            is_list=True,
            temp_file=True,
        ),
    ]
    outputs = [
        Output(display_name="Chat Message", name="message", method="message_response"),
    ]

    async def message_response(self) -> Message:
        # Ensure files is a list and filter out empty/None values
        files = self.files if self.files else []
        if files and not isinstance(files, list):
            files = [files]
        # Filter out None/empty values
        files = [f for f in files if f is not None and f != ""]

        session_id = self.session_id or self.graph.session_id or ""
        message = await Message.create(
            text=self.input_value,
            sender=self.sender,
            sender_name=self.sender_name,
            session_id=session_id,
            context_id=self.context_id,
            files=files,
        )
        if session_id and isinstance(message, Message) and self.should_store_message:
            stored_message = await self.send_message(
                message,
            )
            self.message.value = stored_message
            message = stored_message

        self.status = message
        return message

from typing import Any

from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from pydantic.v1 import SecretStr

from lfx.base.models.model import LCModelComponent
from lfx.base.models.openai_constants import OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL_NAMES, OPENAI_REASONING_MODEL_NAMES
from lfx.field_typing import LanguageModel
from lfx.field_typing.range_spec import RangeSpec
from lfx.inputs.inputs import BoolInput, DictInput, DropdownInput, IntInput, SecretStrInput, SliderInput, StrInput
from lfx.log.logger import logger


class OpenAIModelComponent(LCModelComponent):
    display_name = "OpenAI"
    description = "Generates text using OpenAI LLMs."
    icon = "OpenAI"
    name = "OpenAIModel"

    inputs = [
        *LCModelComponent.get_base_inputs(),
        IntInput(
            name="max_tokens",
            display_name="Max Tokens",
            advanced=True,
            info="The maximum number of tokens to generate. Set to 0 for unlimited tokens.",
            range_spec=RangeSpec(min=0, max=128000),
        ),
        DictInput(
            name="model_kwargs",
            display_name="Model Kwargs",
            advanced=True,
            info="Additional keyword arguments to pass to the model.",
        ),
        BoolInput(
            name="json_mode",
            display_name="JSON Mode",
            advanced=True,
            info="If True, it will output JSON regardless of passing a schema.",
        ),
        DropdownInput(
            name="model_name",
            display_name="Model Name",
            advanced=False,
            options=OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL_NAMES + OPENAI_REASONING_MODEL_NAMES,
            value=OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL_NAMES[0],
            combobox=True,
            real_time_refresh=True,
        ),
        StrInput(
            name="openai_api_base",
            display_name="OpenAI API Base",
            advanced=True,
            info="The base URL of the OpenAI API. "
            "Defaults to https://api.openai.com/v1. "
            "You can change this to use other APIs like JinaChat, LocalAI and Prem.",
        ),
        SecretStrInput(
            name="api_key",
            display_name="OpenAI API Key",
            info="The OpenAI API Key to use for the OpenAI model.",
            advanced=False,
            value="OPENAI_API_KEY",
            required=True,
        ),
        SliderInput(
            name="temperature",
            display_name="Temperature",
            value=0.1,
            range_spec=RangeSpec(min=0, max=1, step=0.01),
            show=True,
        ),
        IntInput(
            name="seed",
            display_name="Seed",
            info="The seed controls the reproducibility of the job.",
            advanced=True,
            value=1,
        ),
        IntInput(
            name="max_retries",
            display_name="Max Retries",
            info="The maximum number of retries to make when generating.",
            advanced=True,
            value=5,
        ),
        IntInput(
            name="timeout",
            display_name="Timeout",
            info="The timeout for requests to OpenAI completion API.",
            advanced=True,
            value=700,
        ),
    ]

    def build_model(self) -> LanguageModel:  # type: ignore[type-var]
        logger.debug(f"Executing request with model: {self.model_name}")
        # Handle api_key - it can be string or SecretStr
        api_key_value = None
        if self.api_key:
            logger.debug(f"API key type: {type(self.api_key)}, value: {self.api_key!r}")
            if isinstance(self.api_key, SecretStr):
                api_key_value = self.api_key.get_secret_value()
            else:
                api_key_value = str(self.api_key)
        logger.debug(f"Final api_key_value type: {type(api_key_value)}, value: {'***' if api_key_value else None}")

        # Handle model_kwargs and ensure api_key doesn't conflict
        model_kwargs = self.model_kwargs or {}
        # Remove api_key from model_kwargs if it exists to prevent conflicts
        if "api_key" in model_kwargs:
            logger.warning("api_key found in model_kwargs, removing to prevent conflicts")
            model_kwargs = dict(model_kwargs)  # Make a copy
            del model_kwargs["api_key"]

        parameters = {
            "api_key": api_key_value,
            "model_name": self.model_name,
            "max_tokens": self.max_tokens or None,
            "model_kwargs": model_kwargs,
            "base_url": self.openai_api_base or "https://api.openai.com/v1",
            "max_retries": self.max_retries,
            "timeout": self.timeout,
        }

        # TODO: Revisit if/once parameters are supported for reasoning models
        unsupported_params_for_reasoning_models = ["temperature", "seed"]

        if self.model_name not in OPENAI_REASONING_MODEL_NAMES:
            parameters["temperature"] = self.temperature if self.temperature is not None else 0.1
            parameters["seed"] = self.seed
        else:
            params_str = ", ".join(unsupported_params_for_reasoning_models)
            logger.debug(f"{self.model_name} is a reasoning model, {params_str} are not configurable. Ignoring.")

        # Ensure all parameter values are the correct types
        if isinstance(parameters.get("api_key"), SecretStr):
            parameters["api_key"] = parameters["api_key"].get_secret_value()
        output = ChatOpenAI(**parameters)
        if self.json_mode:
            output = output.bind(response_format={"type": "json_object"})

        return output

    def _get_exception_message(self, e: Exception):
        """Get a message from an OpenAI exception.

        Args:
            e (Exception): The exception to get the message from.

        Returns:
            str: The message from the exception.
        """
        try:
            from openai import BadRequestError
        except ImportError:
            return None
        if isinstance(e, BadRequestError):
            message = e.body.get("message")
            if message:
                return message
        return None

    def update_build_config(self, build_config: dict, field_value: Any, field_name: str | None = None) -> dict:
        if field_name in {"base_url", "model_name", "api_key"} and field_value in OPENAI_REASONING_MODEL_NAMES:
            build_config["temperature"]["show"] = False
            build_config["seed"]["show"] = False
            # Hide system_message for o1 models - currently unsupported
            if field_value.startswith("o1") and "system_message" in build_config:
                build_config["system_message"]["show"] = False
        if field_name in {"base_url", "model_name", "api_key"} and field_value in OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL_NAMES:
            build_config["temperature"]["show"] = True
            build_config["seed"]["show"] = True
            if "system_message" in build_config:
                build_config["system_message"]["show"] = True
        return build_config

Extract invoice facts from the input. Input may be text, an image, or a PDF.
If an image or PDF is attached: analyze it visually using your vision capability and extract all visible invoice fields directly from the document.
If text only: extract from the readable text content.

Rules:
- Use only content visible in the input (text or image).
- Never invent IDs, amounts, dates, vendor names, or PO numbers.
- Unknown or unreadable -> empty + add to missing_critical_fields.
- Return exactly ONE JSON object. No markdown, no explanation.
- Fields: invoice_type_guess, vendor_name, vendor_id_or_tax_id, invoice_number,
  po_number, invoice_date, due_date, currency, amount_subtotal, amount_tax,
  amount_total, payment_terms, ship_to_or_service_location, line_items_summary,
  language, po_signals_detected, nonpo_signals_detected, missing_critical_fields,
  extraction_confidence, extraction_notes.
- extraction_notes: include source quality tag:
  email_text_only | invoice_text_visible | mixed_readable_sources | low_readability_partial | empty_input.
OUTPUT: valid JSON only. No markdown fences.

from lfx.base.prompts.api_utils import process_prompt_template
from lfx.custom.custom_component.component import Component
from lfx.inputs.inputs import DefaultPromptField
from lfx.io import MessageTextInput, Output, PromptInput
from lfx.schema.message import Message
from lfx.template.utils import update_template_values


class PromptComponent(Component):
    display_name: str = "Prompt Template"
    description: str = "Create a prompt template with dynamic variables."
    documentation: str = "https://docs.langflow.org/components-prompts"
    icon = "braces"
    trace_type = "prompt"
    name = "Prompt Template"
    priority = 0  # Set priority to 0 to make it appear first

    inputs = [
        PromptInput(name="template", display_name="Template"),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="tool_placeholder",
            display_name="Tool Placeholder",
            tool_mode=True,
            advanced=True,
            info="A placeholder input for tool mode.",
        ),
    ]

    outputs = [
        Output(display_name="Prompt", name="prompt", method="build_prompt"),
    ]

    async def build_prompt(self) -> Message:
        prompt = Message.from_template(**self._attributes)
        self.status = prompt.text
        return prompt

    def _update_template(self, frontend_node: dict):
        prompt_template = frontend_node["template"]["template"]["value"]
        custom_fields = frontend_node["custom_fields"]
        frontend_node_template = frontend_node["template"]
        _ = process_prompt_template(
            template=prompt_template,
            name="template",
            custom_fields=custom_fields,
            frontend_node_template=frontend_node_template,
        )
        return frontend_node

    async def update_frontend_node(self, new_frontend_node: dict, current_frontend_node: dict):
        """This function is called after the code validation is done."""
        frontend_node = await super().update_frontend_node(new_frontend_node, current_frontend_node)
        template = frontend_node["template"]["template"]["value"]
        # Kept it duplicated for backwards compatibility
        _ = process_prompt_template(
            template=template,
            name="template",
            custom_fields=frontend_node["custom_fields"],
            frontend_node_template=frontend_node["template"],
        )
        # Now that template is updated, we need to grab any values that were set in the current_frontend_node
        # and update the frontend_node with those values
        update_template_values(new_template=frontend_node, previous_template=current_frontend_node["template"])
        return frontend_node

    def _get_fallback_input(self, **kwargs):
        return DefaultPromptField(**kwargs)

Build ROUTE_PACKET from these inputs:

RAW_MESSAGE:
{raw_message}

INVOICE_JSON:
{invoice_json}

VENDOR_MASTER:
{vendor_master}

PO_REGISTER:
{po_register}

NON_PO_POLICY_RULES:
{nonpo_policy_rules}

MATCHING_RULES:
{matching_rules}

Output sections (mandatory):
1) INPUT_PRESENCE
- raw_message, invoice_json, vendor_master, po_register, nonpo_policy_rules, matching_rules: present or empty

2) EXTRACTED_FACTS (source tagged)
- invoice_number, vendor_name, vendor_id_or_tax_id, po_number, currency, amount_total, service_period, requester, extraction_source_tag
- each with source: raw_message | invoice_json | both | missing

3) KNOWN_UNKNOWN_SPLIT
- known_from_raw_message: list
- known_from_extraction: list
- missing_after_merge: list

4) RAW_MESSAGE_EVIDENCE
- nonpo_keywords_found (exact phrases)
- po_keywords_found
- key_identifiers_seen_in_text

5) ROUTING_SIGNALS
- po_signals
- nonpo_signals
- missing_critical_fields

6) DEMO_LOOKUP_CONTEXT
- vendor_lookup_status: exact_match | fuzzy_match | no_match_in_demo_data | table_empty
- vendor_lookup_detail
- po_lookup_status: exact_match | fuzzy_match | no_match_in_demo_data | not_applicable | table_empty
- po_lookup_detail

7) QUALITY_GATE
- extraction_confidence
- extraction_is_low (below 70)
- packet_ready_for_classification
- packet_notes

Rules:
- Factual only. unknown if missing. no hypotheticals.
- If a table input is empty, set lookup status to table_empty.
- Never fabricate lookup matches when table is empty.

system_message.value:
Classify the invoice as PO, NON_PO, or UNCERTAIN using ROUTE_PACKET.

ROUTING DECISION RULES (apply in order):
1. PO: po_number is explicitly present in the raw message OR extraction, AND po_signals exist, AND no strong contradicting nonpo_signals.
   Demo data no_match_in_demo_data is NOT a reason for UNCERTAIN — it means the vendor/PO are real but not in the demo tables.
2. NON_PO: no po_number found, explicit non-PO language present (e.g. 'without a purchase order', 'no PO'), nonpo_signals dominate.
3. UNCERTAIN: only when genuinely ambiguous — both strong PO and NON_PO signals exist simultaneously, OR critical extraction failure.

Rules:
- Never invent vendor names, invoice IDs, PO numbers, amounts, or dates.
- Return exactly ONE JSON object. No markdown, no explanation.
- Keep status fields canonical:
  matched_vendor_status in {exact_match, fuzzy_match, no_match, ambiguous}
  matched_po_status in {exact_match, fuzzy_match, no_match, not_applicable}
- Put explanations only in *_detail fields.

Anti-contradiction:
1) If vendor_name or vendor_id_or_tax_id exists in known_from_raw_message or known_from_extraction,
   do not say "no vendor data provided". Use no_match + detail = no_match_in_demo_data when needed.
2) If po_number is absent or explicitly N/A and nonpo_signals exist,
   set matched_po_status = not_applicable.
3) If demo table is empty, keep canonical status no_match and explain table_empty in detail.

Fields (ALL required — no classification_card here, card is rendered separately):
route_decision, route_confidence, route_rationale,
IMPORTANT: route_confidence MUST be a decimal number between 0.0 and 1.0. NEVER use words like 'high', 'medium', or 'low'. Examples: strong PO = 0.97, moderate = 0.80, uncertain = 0.50.
invoice_number, vendor_name, currency, amount_total, invoice_date,
matched_vendor_status, matched_vendor_status_detail, matched_vendor_candidates,
matched_po_status, matched_po_status_detail, matched_po_candidates,
key_signals (object with po_signals list and nonpo_signals list),
missing_inputs_for_resolution, risk_flags,
requires_human_review (yes/no),
reason_for_review (brief reason if yes — or 'not required').
OUTPUT: valid JSON only. No markdown fences. No classification_card field.

from lfx.base.prompts.api_utils import process_prompt_template
from lfx.custom.custom_component.component import Component
from lfx.inputs.inputs import DefaultPromptField
from lfx.io import MessageTextInput, Output, PromptInput
from lfx.schema.message import Message
from lfx.template.utils import update_template_values


class PromptComponent(Component):
    display_name: str = "Prompt Template"
    description: str = "Create a prompt template with dynamic variables."
    documentation: str = "https://docs.langflow.org/components-prompts"
    icon = "braces"
    trace_type = "prompt"
    name = "Prompt Template"
    priority = 0  # Set priority to 0 to make it appear first

    inputs = [
        PromptInput(name="template", display_name="Template"),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="tool_placeholder",
            display_name="Tool Placeholder",
            tool_mode=True,
            advanced=True,
            info="A placeholder input for tool mode.",
        ),
    ]

    outputs = [
        Output(display_name="Prompt", name="prompt", method="build_prompt"),
    ]

    async def build_prompt(self) -> Message:
        prompt = Message.from_template(**self._attributes)
        self.status = prompt.text
        return prompt

    def _update_template(self, frontend_node: dict):
        prompt_template = frontend_node["template"]["template"]["value"]
        custom_fields = frontend_node["custom_fields"]
        frontend_node_template = frontend_node["template"]
        _ = process_prompt_template(
            template=prompt_template,
            name="template",
            custom_fields=custom_fields,
            frontend_node_template=frontend_node_template,
        )
        return frontend_node

    async def update_frontend_node(self, new_frontend_node: dict, current_frontend_node: dict):
        """This function is called after the code validation is done."""
        frontend_node = await super().update_frontend_node(new_frontend_node, current_frontend_node)
        template = frontend_node["template"]["template"]["value"]
        # Kept it duplicated for backwards compatibility
        _ = process_prompt_template(
            template=template,
            name="template",
            custom_fields=frontend_node["custom_fields"],
            frontend_node_template=frontend_node["template"],
        )
        # Now that template is updated, we need to grab any values that were set in the current_frontend_node
        # and update the frontend_node with those values
        update_template_values(new_template=frontend_node, previous_template=current_frontend_node["template"])
        return frontend_node

    def _get_fallback_input(self, **kwargs):
        return DefaultPromptField(**kwargs)

from typing import Any

from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from pydantic.v1 import SecretStr

from lfx.base.models.model import LCModelComponent
from lfx.base.models.openai_constants import OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL_NAMES, OPENAI_REASONING_MODEL_NAMES
from lfx.field_typing import LanguageModel
from lfx.field_typing.range_spec import RangeSpec
from lfx.inputs.inputs import BoolInput, DictInput, DropdownInput, IntInput, SecretStrInput, SliderInput, StrInput
from lfx.log.logger import logger


class OpenAIModelComponent(LCModelComponent):
    display_name = "OpenAI"
    description = "Generates text using OpenAI LLMs."
    icon = "OpenAI"
    name = "OpenAIModel"

    inputs = [
        *LCModelComponent.get_base_inputs(),
        IntInput(
            name="max_tokens",
            display_name="Max Tokens",
            advanced=True,
            info="The maximum number of tokens to generate. Set to 0 for unlimited tokens.",
            range_spec=RangeSpec(min=0, max=128000),
        ),
        DictInput(
            name="model_kwargs",
            display_name="Model Kwargs",
            advanced=True,
            info="Additional keyword arguments to pass to the model.",
        ),
        BoolInput(
            name="json_mode",
            display_name="JSON Mode",
            advanced=True,
            info="If True, it will output JSON regardless of passing a schema.",
        ),
        DropdownInput(
            name="model_name",
            display_name="Model Name",
            advanced=False,
            options=OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL_NAMES + OPENAI_REASONING_MODEL_NAMES,
            value=OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL_NAMES[0],
            combobox=True,
            real_time_refresh=True,
        ),
        StrInput(
            name="openai_api_base",
            display_name="OpenAI API Base",
            advanced=True,
            info="The base URL of the OpenAI API. "
            "Defaults to https://api.openai.com/v1. "
            "You can change this to use other APIs like JinaChat, LocalAI and Prem.",
        ),
        SecretStrInput(
            name="api_key",
            display_name="OpenAI API Key",
            info="The OpenAI API Key to use for the OpenAI model.",
            advanced=False,
            value="OPENAI_API_KEY",
            required=True,
        ),
        SliderInput(
            name="temperature",
            display_name="Temperature",
            value=0.1,
            range_spec=RangeSpec(min=0, max=1, step=0.01),
            show=True,
        ),
        IntInput(
            name="seed",
            display_name="Seed",
            info="The seed controls the reproducibility of the job.",
            advanced=True,
            value=1,
        ),
        IntInput(
            name="max_retries",
            display_name="Max Retries",
            info="The maximum number of retries to make when generating.",
            advanced=True,
            value=5,
        ),
        IntInput(
            name="timeout",
            display_name="Timeout",
            info="The timeout for requests to OpenAI completion API.",
            advanced=True,
            value=700,
        ),
    ]

    def build_model(self) -> LanguageModel:  # type: ignore[type-var]
        logger.debug(f"Executing request with model: {self.model_name}")
        # Handle api_key - it can be string or SecretStr
        api_key_value = None
        if self.api_key:
            logger.debug(f"API key type: {type(self.api_key)}, value: {self.api_key!r}")
            if isinstance(self.api_key, SecretStr):
                api_key_value = self.api_key.get_secret_value()
            else:
                api_key_value = str(self.api_key)
        logger.debug(f"Final api_key_value type: {type(api_key_value)}, value: {'***' if api_key_value else None}")

        # Handle model_kwargs and ensure api_key doesn't conflict
        model_kwargs = self.model_kwargs or {}
        # Remove api_key from model_kwargs if it exists to prevent conflicts
        if "api_key" in model_kwargs:
            logger.warning("api_key found in model_kwargs, removing to prevent conflicts")
            model_kwargs = dict(model_kwargs)  # Make a copy
            del model_kwargs["api_key"]

        parameters = {
            "api_key": api_key_value,
            "model_name": self.model_name,
            "max_tokens": self.max_tokens or None,
            "model_kwargs": model_kwargs,
            "base_url": self.openai_api_base or "https://api.openai.com/v1",
            "max_retries": self.max_retries,
            "timeout": self.timeout,
        }

        # TODO: Revisit if/once parameters are supported for reasoning models
        unsupported_params_for_reasoning_models = ["temperature", "seed"]

        if self.model_name not in OPENAI_REASONING_MODEL_NAMES:
            parameters["temperature"] = self.temperature if self.temperature is not None else 0.1
            parameters["seed"] = self.seed
        else:
            params_str = ", ".join(unsupported_params_for_reasoning_models)
            logger.debug(f"{self.model_name} is a reasoning model, {params_str} are not configurable. Ignoring.")

        # Ensure all parameter values are the correct types
        if isinstance(parameters.get("api_key"), SecretStr):
            parameters["api_key"] = parameters["api_key"].get_secret_value()
        output = ChatOpenAI(**parameters)
        if self.json_mode:
            output = output.bind(response_format={"type": "json_object"})

        return output

    def _get_exception_message(self, e: Exception):
        """Get a message from an OpenAI exception.

        Args:
            e (Exception): The exception to get the message from.

        Returns:
            str: The message from the exception.
        """
        try:
            from openai import BadRequestError
        except ImportError:
            return None
        if isinstance(e, BadRequestError):
            message = e.body.get("message")
            if message:
                return message
        return None

    def update_build_config(self, build_config: dict, field_value: Any, field_name: str | None = None) -> dict:
        if field_name in {"base_url", "model_name", "api_key"} and field_value in OPENAI_REASONING_MODEL_NAMES:
            build_config["temperature"]["show"] = False
            build_config["seed"]["show"] = False
            # Hide system_message for o1 models - currently unsupported
            if field_value.startswith("o1") and "system_message" in build_config:
                build_config["system_message"]["show"] = False
        if field_name in {"base_url", "model_name", "api_key"} and field_value in OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL_NAMES:
            build_config["temperature"]["show"] = True
            build_config["seed"]["show"] = True
            if "system_message" in build_config:
                build_config["system_message"]["show"] = True
        return build_config

system_message.value:
You receive a routing decision JSON from a PO vs Non-PO classifier.
Render a classification card using ONLY the values in this JSON. Do NOT invent any value. If a field is missing: write unknown.

STEP 1 — Extract AND compute these values from the JSON:
- invoice_number, vendor_name, currency, amount_total, invoice_date
- route_decision, route_rationale
- confidence_pct: read route_confidence and convert to an integer percentage:
  * If it is a number <= 1.0 → multiply by 100 and round (e.g. 0.99 → 99, 0.80 → 80)
  * If it is a number > 1.0 → round to nearest integer (e.g. 95.0 → 95)
  * If it is the string 'high' → use 95
  * If it is the string 'medium' → use 75
  * If it is the string 'low' → use 55
  * If it is missing, null, or 'unknown' → infer from evidence: strong PO match = 95, NON_PO with policy = 80, UNCERTAIN = 55
  NEVER output 'unknown%'. Always produce an integer.
- confidence_label: if confidence_pct >= 90 → High; if 70-89 → Medium; if below 70 → Low
- key_signals.po_signals (list), key_signals.nonpo_signals (list)
- matched_vendor_status, matched_vendor_status_detail
- matched_po_status, matched_po_status_detail
- risk_flags
- human_review: capitalize requires_human_review → 'Yes' or 'No'
- reason_for_review

STEP 2 — Output the Viewer Guide first (exactly as shown), then the card.

VIEWER GUIDE
Purpose: Instant routing decision for AP intake
Produced by: C classifier · vendor/PO master data checks
What to look at: Verdict · Confidence · Human Review gate
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

---START OF CARD---
CLASSIFICATION CARD — PO vs NON-PO ROUTING DECISION

INVOICE: <invoice_number> | <vendor_name> | <currency> <amount_total> | <invoice_date>

VERDICT: <route_decision>
CONFIDENCE: <confidence_pct>% (<confidence_label>)

KEY EVIDENCE
PO signals:
<one signal per bullet line from key_signals.po_signals — or '- None detected'>

NON_PO signals:
<one signal per bullet line from key_signals.nonpo_signals — or '- None detected'>

DATA MATCHES
- Vendor match: <matched_vendor_status> | <matched_vendor_status_detail>
- PO match: <matched_po_status> | <matched_po_status_detail>

RISKS OR CAVEATS
<risk_flags as bullet list — one item per line>

ROUTING RATIONALE
<route_rationale — 2-4 sentences. Plain business language.>

HUMAN REVIEW REQUIRED: <human_review>
REASON: <reason_for_review>
---END OF CARD---

Output Viewer Guide + card text only. No JSON. No explanation. No markdown fences.

            show=True,
        ),
        IntInput(
            name="seed",
            display_name="Seed",
            info="The seed controls the reproducibility of the job.",
            advanced=True,
            value=1,
        ),
        IntInput(
            name="max_retries",
            display_name="Max Retries",
            info="The maximum number of retries to make when generating.",
            advanced=True,
            value=5,
        ),
        IntInput(
            name="timeout",
            display_name="Timeout",
            info="The timeout for requests to OpenAI completion API.",
            advanced=True,
            value=700,

    ]

                api_key_value = self.api_key.get_secret_value()
            else:
                api_key_value = str(self.api_key)
        logger.debug(f"Final api_key_value type: {type(api_key_value)}, value: {'***' if api_key_value else None}")

        # Handle model_kwargs and ensure api_key doesn't conflict
        model_kwargs = self.model_kwargs or {}
        # Remove api_key from model_kwargs if it exists to prevent conflicts
        if "api_key" in model_kwargs:
            logger.warning("api_key found in model_kwargs, removing to prevent conflicts")
            model_kwargs = dict(model_kwargs)  # Make a copy
            del model_kwargs["api_key"]

        parameters = {
            "api_key": api_key_value,

        unsupported_params_for_reasoning_models = ["temperature", "seed"]

        if self.model_name not in OPENAI_REASONING_MODEL_NAMES:
            parameters["temperature"] = self.temperature if self.temperature is not None else 0.1
            parameters["seed"] = self.seed
        else:
            params_str = ", ".join(unsupported_params_for_reasoning_models)

        # Ensure all parameter values are the correct types
        if isinstance(parameters.get("api_key"), SecretStr):
            parameters["api_key"] = parameters["api_key"].get_secret_value()
        output = ChatOpenAI(**parameters)
        if self.json_mode:
            output = output.bind(response_format={"type": "json_object"})

        return output

    def _get_exception_message(self, e: Exception):
        """Get a message from an OpenAI exception.

            e (Exception): The exception to get the message from.

        Returns:
            str: The message from the exception.
        """
        try:
            from openai import BadRequestError
        except ImportError:
            return None
        if isinstance(e, BadRequestError):
            message = e.body.get("message")
            if message:
                return message
        return None

    def update_build_config(self, build_config: dict, field_value: Any, field_name: str | None = None) -> dict:
        if field_name in {"base_url", "model_name", "api_key"} and field_value in OPENAI_REASONING_MODEL_NAMES:
            build_config["temperature"]["show"] = False
            build_config["seed"]["show"] = False
            # Hide system_message for o1 models - currently unsupported
            if field_value.startswith("o1") and "system_message" in build_config:
                build_config["system_message"]["show"] = False
        if field_name in {"base_url", "model_name", "api_key"} and field_value in OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL_NAMES:
            build_config["temperature"]["show"] = True
            build_config["seed"]["show"] = True
            if "system_message" in build_config:
                build_config["system_message"]["show"] = True
        return build_config

from lfx.base.prompts.api_utils import process_prompt_template
from lfx.custom.custom_component.component import Component
from lfx.inputs.inputs import DefaultPromptField
from lfx.io import MessageTextInput, Output, PromptInput
from lfx.schema.message import Message
from lfx.template.utils import update_template_values


class PromptComponent(Component):
    display_name: str = "Prompt Template"
    description: str = "Create a prompt template with dynamic variables."
    documentation: str = "https://docs.langflow.org/components-prompts"
    icon = "braces"
    trace_type = "prompt"
    name = "Prompt Template"
    priority = 0  # Set priority to 0 to make it appear first

    inputs = [
        PromptInput(name="template", display_name="Template"),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="tool_placeholder",
            display_name="Tool Placeholder",
            tool_mode=True,
            advanced=True,
            info="A placeholder input for tool mode.",
        ),
    ]

    outputs = [
        Output(display_name="Prompt", name="prompt", method="build_prompt"),
    ]

    async def build_prompt(self) -> Message:
        prompt = Message.from_template(**self._attributes)
        self.status = prompt.text
        return prompt

    def _update_template(self, frontend_node: dict):
        prompt_template = frontend_node["template"]["template"]["value"]
        custom_fields = frontend_node["custom_fields"]
        frontend_node_template = frontend_node["template"]
        _ = process_prompt_template(
            template=prompt_template,
            name="template",
            custom_fields=custom_fields,
            frontend_node_template=frontend_node_template,
        )
        return frontend_node

    async def update_frontend_node(self, new_frontend_node: dict, current_frontend_node: dict):
        """This function is called after the code validation is done."""
        frontend_node = await super().update_frontend_node(new_frontend_node, current_frontend_node)
        template = frontend_node["template"]["template"]["value"]
        # Kept it duplicated for backwards compatibility
        _ = process_prompt_template(
            template=template,
            name="template",
            custom_fields=frontend_node["custom_fields"],
            frontend_node_template=frontend_node["template"],
        )
        # Now that template is updated, we need to grab any values that were set in the current_frontend_node
        # and update the frontend_node with those values
        update_template_values(new_template=frontend_node, previous_template=current_frontend_node["template"])
        return frontend_node

    def _get_fallback_input(self, **kwargs):
        return DefaultPromptField(**kwargs)

system_message.value:
You receive a PIPELINE DATA PACKET with 5 labelled JSON sections from an AP invoice workflow.
Produce a FINAL_CONTEXT document with exactly 6 sections.
Downstream outputs depend on FINAL_CONTEXT being accurate — they look for specific field names.

EXTRACTION RULES (no exceptions):
- Extract each value from the exact JSON path listed below.
- If a field is null, empty, absent, or 'n/a': write the word unknown (no quotes).
- Do NOT infer, guess, fill in, or substitute. unknown means unknown.
- Do NOT normalize vendor names, IDs, amounts. Preserve exactly.
- branch_applicability = not_applicable: label that branch 'branch not executed'.

SECTION 1 — LOCKED_FACTS
One key: value pair per line. No pipe separators. Exact format:
vendor_name: [INVOICE_JSON.vendor_name]
vendor_id: [INVOICE_JSON.vendor_id_or_tax_id]
invoice_number: [INVOICE_JSON.invoice_number]
po_number: [INVOICE_JSON.po_number]
currency: [INVOICE_JSON.currency]
amount_total: [INVOICE_JSON.amount_total]
invoice_date: [INVOICE_JSON.invoice_date]
due_date: [INVOICE_JSON.due_date]
route_decision: [ROUTE_JSON.route_decision]
route_confidence: [ROUTE_JSON.route_confidence]
active_branch: [ROUTE_JSON.route_decision]
requires_human_review: [ROUTE_JSON.requires_human_review]
reason_for_review: [ROUTE_JSON.reason_for_review]

SECTION 2 — CHECKS_PERFORMED
Header line: check_name|result|source|notes
One pipe-delimited row per check:
vendor_master_match | [ROUTE_JSON.matched_vendor_status] | ROUTE_JSON | [ROUTE_JSON.matched_vendor_status_detail]
po_register_match | [ROUTE_JSON.matched_po_status] | ROUTE_JSON | [ROUTE_JSON.matched_po_status_detail]
currency_match | [PO_RESOLUTION_JSON.amount_tolerance_result, or not_executed] | PO_RESOLUTION_JSON | [PO_RESOLUTION_JSON.amount_tolerance_detail]
amount_tolerance | [PO_RESOLUTION_JSON.amount_tolerance_result, or not_executed] | PO_RESOLUTION_JSON | [detail]
duplicate_risk | [extract from ROUTE_JSON.risk_flags, or not_flagged] | ROUTE_JSON | [notes]
approval_routing | [NONPO_RESOLUTION_JSON.approval_route_suggestion, or not_applicable] | NONPO_RESOLUTION_JSON | [detail]
service_acceptance | [PO_RESOLUTION_JSON.gr_status_assessment, or not_executed] | PO_RESOLUTION_JSON | [detail]

SECTION 3 — ASSUMPTIONS
Header line: assumption|impact|risk
Source: ROUTE_JSON.missing_inputs_for_resolution + INVOICE_JSON.missing_critical_fields.
If none: one row: none assumed|none|low

SECTION 4 — HUMAN_GATES
Header line: gate|reason|urgency
Source: ROUTE_JSON.requires_human_review + reason_for_review.
Add gates from PO_RESOLUTION_JSON.po_next_steps or NONPO_RESOLUTION_JSON.nonpo_next_steps that mention human action.
If none: one row: none required|all checks auto-passed|low

SECTION 5 — ACTIVE_BRANCH_SUMMARY
3-5 sentences. Source by active branch:
PO → PO_RESOLUTION_JSON.po_resolution_summary + po_risk_flags
NON_PO → NONPO_RESOLUTION_JSON.nonpo_resolution_summary + nonpo_risk_flags
UNCERTAIN → ROUTE_JSON.route_rationale
Inactive branch: write 'branch not executed'.

SECTION 6 — DEMO_LIMITATIONS
Write exactly these 5 bullets:

from typing import Any

from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from pydantic.v1 import SecretStr

from lfx.base.models.model import LCModelComponent
from lfx.base.models.openai_constants import OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL_NAMES, OPENAI_REASONING_MODEL_NAMES
from lfx.field_typing import LanguageModel
from lfx.field_typing.range_spec import RangeSpec
from lfx.inputs.inputs import BoolInput, DictInput, DropdownInput, IntInput, SecretStrInput, SliderInput, StrInput
from lfx.log.logger import logger


class OpenAIModelComponent(LCModelComponent):
    display_name = "OpenAI"
    description = "Generates text using OpenAI LLMs."
    icon = "OpenAI"
    name = "OpenAIModel"

    inputs = [
        *LCModelComponent.get_base_inputs(),
        IntInput(
            name="max_tokens",
            display_name="Max Tokens",
            advanced=True,
            info="The maximum number of tokens to generate. Set to 0 for unlimited tokens.",
            range_spec=RangeSpec(min=0, max=128000),
        ),
        DictInput(
            name="model_kwargs",
            display_name="Model Kwargs",
            advanced=True,
            info="Additional keyword arguments to pass to the model.",
        ),
        BoolInput(
            name="json_mode",
            display_name="JSON Mode",
            advanced=True,
            info="If True, it will output JSON regardless of passing a schema.",
        ),
        DropdownInput(
            name="model_name",
            display_name="Model Name",
            advanced=False,
            options=OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL_NAMES + OPENAI_REASONING_MODEL_NAMES,
            value=OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL_NAMES[0],
            combobox=True,
            real_time_refresh=True,
        ),
        StrInput(
            name="openai_api_base",
            display_name="OpenAI API Base",
            advanced=True,
            info="The base URL of the OpenAI API. "
            "Defaults to https://api.openai.com/v1. "
            "You can change this to use other APIs like JinaChat, LocalAI and Prem.",
        ),
        SecretStrInput(
            name="api_key",
            display_name="OpenAI API Key",
            info="The OpenAI API Key to use for the OpenAI model.",
            advanced=False,
            value="OPENAI_API_KEY",
            required=True,
        ),
        SliderInput(
            name="temperature",
            display_name="Temperature",
            value=0.1,
            range_spec=RangeSpec(min=0, max=1, step=0.01),
            show=True,
        ),
        IntInput(
            name="seed",
            display_name="Seed",
            info="The seed controls the reproducibility of the job.",
            advanced=True,
            value=1,
        ),
        IntInput(
            name="max_retries",
            display_name="Max Retries",
            info="The maximum number of retries to make when generating.",
            advanced=True,
            value=5,
        ),
        IntInput(
            name="timeout",
            display_name="Timeout",
            info="The timeout for requests to OpenAI completion API.",
            advanced=True,
            value=700,
        ),
    ]

    def build_model(self) -> LanguageModel:  # type: ignore[type-var]
        logger.debug(f"Executing request with model: {self.model_name}")
        # Handle api_key - it can be string or SecretStr
        api_key_value = None
        if self.api_key:
            logger.debug(f"API key type: {type(self.api_key)}, value: {self.api_key!r}")
            if isinstance(self.api_key, SecretStr):
                api_key_value = self.api_key.get_secret_value()
            else:
                api_key_value = str(self.api_key)
        logger.debug(f"Final api_key_value type: {type(api_key_value)}, value: {'***' if api_key_value else None}")

        # Handle model_kwargs and ensure api_key doesn't conflict
        model_kwargs = self.model_kwargs or {}
        # Remove api_key from model_kwargs if it exists to prevent conflicts
        if "api_key" in model_kwargs:
            logger.warning("api_key found in model_kwargs, removing to prevent conflicts")
            model_kwargs = dict(model_kwargs)  # Make a copy
            del model_kwargs["api_key"]

        parameters = {
            "api_key": api_key_value,
            "model_name": self.model_name,
            "max_tokens": self.max_tokens or None,
            "model_kwargs": model_kwargs,
            "base_url": self.openai_api_base or "https://api.openai.com/v1",
            "max_retries": self.max_retries,
            "timeout": self.timeout,
        }

        # TODO: Revisit if/once parameters are supported for reasoning models
        unsupported_params_for_reasoning_models = ["temperature", "seed"]

        if self.model_name not in OPENAI_REASONING_MODEL_NAMES:
            parameters["temperature"] = self.temperature if self.temperature is not None else 0.1
            parameters["seed"] = self.seed
        else:
            params_str = ", ".join(unsupported_params_for_reasoning_models)
            logger.debug(f"{self.model_name} is a reasoning model, {params_str} are not configurable. Ignoring.")

        # Ensure all parameter values are the correct types
        if isinstance(parameters.get("api_key"), SecretStr):
            parameters["api_key"] = parameters["api_key"].get_secret_value()
        output = ChatOpenAI(**parameters)
        if self.json_mode:
            output = output.bind(response_format={"type": "json_object"})

        return output

    def _get_exception_message(self, e: Exception):
        """Get a message from an OpenAI exception.

        Args:
            e (Exception): The exception to get the message from.

        Returns:
            str: The message from the exception.
        """
        try:
            from openai import BadRequestError
        except ImportError:
            return None
        if isinstance(e, BadRequestError):
            message = e.body.get("message")
            if message:
                return message
        return None

    def update_build_config(self, build_config: dict, field_value: Any, field_name: str | None = None) -> dict:
        if field_name in {"base_url", "model_name", "api_key"} and field_value in OPENAI_REASONING_MODEL_NAMES:
            build_config["temperature"]["show"] = False
            build_config["seed"]["show"] = False
            # Hide system_message for o1 models - currently unsupported
            if field_value.startswith("o1") and "system_message" in build_config:
                build_config["system_message"]["show"] = False
        if field_name in {"base_url", "model_name", "api_key"} and field_value in OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL_NAMES:
            build_config["temperature"]["show"] = True
            build_config["seed"]["show"] = True
            if "system_message" in build_config:
                build_config["system_message"]["show"] = True
        return build_config

from lfx.base.models.model import LCModelComponent
from lfx.base.models.openai_constants import OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL_NAMES, OPENAI_REASONING_MODEL_NAMES
from lfx.field_typing import LanguageModel
from lfx.field_typing.range_spec import RangeSpec
from lfx.inputs.inputs import BoolInput, DictInput, DropdownInput, IntInput, SecretStrInput, SliderInput, StrInput
from lfx.log.logger import logger


class OpenAIModelComponent(LCModelComponent):
    display_name = "OpenAI"
    description = "Generates text using OpenAI LLMs."
    icon = "OpenAI"
    name = "OpenAIModel"

    inputs = [
        *LCModelComponent.get_base_inputs(),
        IntInput(
            name="max_tokens",
            display_name="Max Tokens",
            advanced=True,
            info="The maximum number of tokens to generate. Set to 0 for unlimited tokens.",
            range_spec=RangeSpec(min=0, max=128000),
        ),
        DictInput(
            name="model_kwargs",
            display_name="Model Kwargs",
            advanced=True,
            info="Additional keyword arguments to pass to the model.",
        ),
        BoolInput(
            name="json_mode",
            display_name="JSON Mode",
            advanced=True,
            info="If True, it will output JSON regardless of passing a schema.",
        ),
        DropdownInput(
            name="model_name",
            display_name="Model Name",
            advanced=False,
            options=OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL_NAMES + OPENAI_REASONING_MODEL_NAMES,
            value=OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL_NAMES[0],
            combobox=True,
            real_time_refresh=True,
        ),
        StrInput(
            name="openai_api_base",
            display_name="OpenAI API Base",
            advanced=True,
            info="The base URL of the OpenAI API. "
            "Defaults to https://api.openai.com/v1. "
            "You can change this to use other APIs like JinaChat, LocalAI and Prem.",
        ),
        SecretStrInput(
            name="api_key",
            display_name="OpenAI API Key",
            info="The OpenAI API Key to use for the OpenAI model.",
            advanced=False,
            value="OPENAI_API_KEY",
            required=True,
        ),
        SliderInput(
            name="temperature",
            display_name="Temperature",
            value=0.1,
            range_spec=RangeSpec(min=0, max=1, step=0.01),
            show=True,
        ),
        IntInput(
            name="seed",
            display_name="Seed",
            info="The seed controls the reproducibility of the job.",
            advanced=True,
            value=1,
        ),
        IntInput(
            name="max_retries",
            display_name="Max Retries",
            info="The maximum number of retries to make when generating.",
            advanced=True,
            value=5,
        ),
        IntInput(
            name="timeout",
            display_name="Timeout",
            info="The timeout for requests to OpenAI completion API.",
            advanced=True,
            value=700,
        ),
    ]

    def build_model(self) -> LanguageModel:  # type: ignore[type-var]
        logger.debug(f"Executing request with model: {self.model_name}")
        # Handle api_key - it can be string or SecretStr
        api_key_value = None
        if self.api_key:
            logger.debug(f"API key type: {type(self.api_key)}, value: {self.api_key!r}")
            if isinstance(self.api_key, SecretStr):
                api_key_value = self.api_key.get_secret_value()
            else:
                api_key_value = str(self.api_key)
        logger.debug(f"Final api_key_value type: {type(api_key_value)}, value: {'***' if api_key_value else None}")

        # Handle model_kwargs and ensure api_key doesn't conflict
        model_kwargs = self.model_kwargs or {}
        # Remove api_key from model_kwargs if it exists to prevent conflicts
        if "api_key" in model_kwargs:
            logger.warning("api_key found in model_kwargs, removing to prevent conflicts")
            model_kwargs = dict(model_kwargs)  # Make a copy
            del model_kwargs["api_key"]

        parameters = {
            "api_key": api_key_value,
            "model_name": self.model_name,
            "max_tokens": self.max_tokens or None,
            "model_kwargs": model_kwargs,
            "base_url": self.openai_api_base or "https://api.openai.com/v1",
            "max_retries": self.max_retries,
            "timeout": self.timeout,
        }

        # TODO: Revisit if/once parameters are supported for reasoning models
        unsupported_params_for_reasoning_models = ["temperature", "seed"]

        if self.model_name not in OPENAI_REASONING_MODEL_NAMES:
            parameters["temperature"] = self.temperature if self.temperature is not None else 0.1
            parameters["seed"] = self.seed
        else:
            params_str = ", ".join(unsupported_params_for_reasoning_models)
            logger.debug(f"{self.model_name} is a reasoning model, {params_str} are not configurable. Ignoring.")

        # Ensure all parameter values are the correct types
        if isinstance(parameters.get("api_key"), SecretStr):
            parameters["api_key"] = parameters["api_key"].get_secret_value()
        output = ChatOpenAI(**parameters)
        if self.json_mode:
            output = output.bind(response_format={"type": "json_object"})

        return output

    def _get_exception_message(self, e: Exception):
        """Get a message from an OpenAI exception.

        Args:
            e (Exception): The exception to get the message from.

        Returns:
            str: The message from the exception.
        """
        try:
            from openai import BadRequestError
        except ImportError:
            return None
        if isinstance(e, BadRequestError):
            message = e.body.get("message")
            if message:
                return message
        return None

    def update_build_config(self, build_config: dict, field_value: Any, field_name: str | None = None) -> dict:
        if field_name in {"base_url", "model_name", "api_key"} and field_value in OPENAI_REASONING_MODEL_NAMES:
            build_config["temperature"]["show"] = False
            build_config["seed"]["show"] = False
            # Hide system_message for o1 models - currently unsupported
            if field_value.startswith("o1") and "system_message" in build_config:
                build_config["system_message"]["show"] = False
        if field_name in {"base_url", "model_name", "api_key"} and field_value in OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL_NAMES:
            build_config["temperature"]["show"] = True
            build_config["seed"]["show"] = True
            if "system_message" in build_config:
                build_config["system_message"]["show"] = True
        return build_config

VIEWER GUIDE
Purpose: Concrete action checklist for the AP Clerk running this invoice
Produced by: Active branch · open items · human gates from FINAL_CONTEXT
What to look at: Phase 1 critical blockers · Owner · Due timing
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FINAL_CONTEXT NAVIGATION (read second — then generate your table):
FINAL_CONTEXT has 6 named sections. Use these sources:
- BRANCH DETECTION → LOCKED_FACTS 'active_branch: ...' line (values: PO, NON_PO, UNCERTAIN)
- Entity data for action text:

  invoice number → LOCKED_FACTS 'invoice_number: ...' line
  PO number → LOCKED_FACTS 'po_number: ...' line
  amount → LOCKED_FACTS 'amount_total: ...' line
  currency → LOCKED_FACTS 'currency: ...' line
- Blocking actions → HUMAN_GATES section
- Evidence requirements → CHECKS_PERFORMED section
If a LOCKED_FACTS value is 'unknown': write exactly: Not available in this run
NEVER substitute a different vendor, invoice number, PO number, or amount.

You are an AP Operational Action Checklist Generator.

Audience: AP Clerk / AP Operations user
Goal: Produce a concrete, branch-aware checklist for the current invoice run using real entities from FINAL_CONTEXT (vendor, PO, amount, currency, etc.). This checklist must be immediately actionable.

INPUT
You will receive FINAL_CONTEXT for a single invoice run.

STRICT GROUNDING RULES
1) Use ONLY facts explicitly present in FINAL_CONTEXT.
2) Do NOT invent vendor names, PO numbers, amounts, dates, approvers, or statuses.
3) If any required detail is missing, write exactly: Not available in this run
4) Include real entity references in the action text whenever available (vendor, PO, amount, currency, invoice number).
5) Checklist must be branch-aware:
   - PO path
   - NON_PO path
   - UNCERTAIN / EXCEPTION path
6) Do not output generic advice. Every row must be a concrete action or a concrete 'no action required' statement tied to this run.

OUTPUT FORMAT — Markdown hierarchical phases (NOT a pipe table)

## 📋 ACTION CHECKLIST — <vendor_name>
**Invoice:** <invoice_number> | **Amount:** <currency> <amount_total> | **Branch:** <active_branch> | **Human Review:** <human_review>

---

## 🔴 PHASE 1: CRITICAL BLOCKERS (P1)
*These actions must be completed before posting to ERP.*

[ACTION_ID] **Action title (1 clear phrase)**
- **Action:** [explicit sentence with real entity references]
- **Owner:** [role]
- **Evidence:** [what to collect]
- **Due:** [timing — e.g. same day / before posting / before payment run]
- **Status:** ⏳ PENDING

[one block per P1 action]

---

## 🟡 PHASE 2: COMPLIANCE & CONTROL (P2)
*Risk mitigation and master data validation.*

[same block structure as Phase 1]

---

## 🔵 PHASE 3: FOLLOW-UP & ESCALATION (P3)
*Post-process tasks or contingency actions.*

[same block structure as Phase 1]

FORMATTING RULES:
- Do NOT use pipe-delimited tables. Do NOT use | characters in action content.
- One action block per action. Minimum 6 total actions across all phases.
- Action IDs: NONPO_NNN for NON_PO branch · PO_NNN for PO branch · EXC_NNN for UNCERTAIN
- Only include phases that have at least one action for this run.
- Status values: ⏳ PENDING · ✅ COMPLETED · ⚠️ ESCALATE · ➖ NOT REQUIRED
- Owner values: AP Clerk / AP Manager / Requester / Cost Center Manager / Finance Director / Logistics Coordinator / Procurement / IT Support

BRANCH LOGIC (apply only if supported by FINAL_CONTEXT)
A) If branch = PO:
- Include checks/actions for vendor master match, PO existence, currency match, tolerance review, duplicate check
- For goods: include GR requirement
- For services: acceptance note can be sufficient if GR not required
- Include route/post/approval action if no blocking exception remains
- If all checks passed, include a 'touchless candidate confirmation' action (non-blocking) or 'No further action required' row with status = Completed

B) If branch = NON_PO:
- Include required coding and approval actions (cost center + GL + requester + manager approval)
- Include contract/SOW reference check for recurring services if applicable
- Include VAT validation action (e.g., ES VAT 21%) if country/tax context supports it
- Include duplicate check and vendor active status check
- Include escalation row if required data is missing

C) If branch = UNCERTAIN or EXCEPTION:
- Include actions to resolve classification/mismatch first
- Include named escalation target (AP Manager or relevant owner)
- Include data chase actions (missing PO, vendor mismatch, missing amount/currency, missing evidence)
- Do not include posting/release actions until blockers are cleared

QUALITY RULES
- Action text must be explicit and concise.
- Example style: 'Validate PO 4502573161 is open and matches currency USD before posting invoice SF-2026-991'
- If a field is unavailable, still create the action and write 'Not available in this run' in the Evidence sub-bullet
- No duplicate rows

LANGUAGE RULES
- Use process language: 'the workflow flagged', 'the run identified', 'a checkpoint remains'.
- Never write 'the AI reasoned', 'I determined', or 'the model concluded'.

from collections.abc import Generator
from typing import Any

import orjson
from fastapi.encoders import jsonable_encoder

from lfx.base.io.chat import ChatComponent
from lfx.helpers.data import safe_convert
from lfx.inputs.inputs import BoolInput, DropdownInput, HandleInput, MessageTextInput
from lfx.schema.data import Data
from lfx.schema.dataframe import DataFrame
from lfx.schema.message import Message
from lfx.schema.properties import Source
from lfx.template.field.base import Output
from lfx.utils.constants import (
    MESSAGE_SENDER_AI,
    MESSAGE_SENDER_NAME_AI,
    MESSAGE_SENDER_USER,
)


class ChatOutput(ChatComponent):
    display_name = "Chat Output"
    description = "Display a chat message in the Playground."
    documentation: str = "https://docs.langflow.org/chat-input-and-output"
    icon = "MessagesSquare"
    name = "ChatOutput"
    minimized = True

    inputs = [
        HandleInput(
            name="input_value",
            display_name="Inputs",
            info="Message to be passed as output.",
            input_types=["Data", "DataFrame", "Message"],
            required=True,
        ),
        BoolInput(
            name="should_store_message",
            display_name="Store Messages",
            info="Store the message in the history.",
            value=True,
            advanced=True,
        ),
        DropdownInput(
            name="sender",
            display_name="Sender Type",
            options=[MESSAGE_SENDER_AI, MESSAGE_SENDER_USER],
            value=MESSAGE_SENDER_AI,
            advanced=True,
            info="Type of sender.",
        ),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="sender_name",
            display_name="Sender Name",
            info="Name of the sender.",
            value=MESSAGE_SENDER_NAME_AI,
            advanced=True,
        ),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="session_id",
            display_name="Session ID",
            info="The session ID of the chat. If empty, the current session ID parameter will be used.",
            advanced=True,
        ),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="context_id",
            display_name="Context ID",
            info="The context ID of the chat. Adds an extra layer to the local memory.",
            value="",
            advanced=True,
        ),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="data_template",
            display_name="Data Template",
            value="{text}",
            advanced=True,
            info="Template to convert Data to Text. If left empty, it will be dynamically set to the Data's text key.",
        ),
        BoolInput(
            name="clean_data",
            display_name="Basic Clean Data",
            value=True,
            advanced=True,
            info="Whether to clean data before converting to string.",
        ),
    ]
    outputs = [
        Output(
            display_name="Output Message",
            name="message",
            method="message_response",
        ),
    ]

    def _build_source(self, id_: str | None, display_name: str | None, source: str | None) -> Source:
        source_dict = {}
        if id_:
            source_dict["id"] = id_
        if display_name:
            source_dict["display_name"] = display_name
        if source:
            # Handle case where source is a ChatOpenAI object
            if hasattr(source, "model_name"):
                source_dict["source"] = source.model_name
            elif hasattr(source, "model"):
                source_dict["source"] = str(source.model)
            else:
                source_dict["source"] = str(source)
        return Source(**source_dict)

    async def message_response(self) -> Message:
        # First convert the input to string if needed
        text = self.convert_to_string()

        # Get source properties
        source, _, display_name, source_id = self.get_properties_from_source_component()

        # Create or use existing Message object
        if isinstance(self.input_value, Message) and not self.is_connected_to_chat_input():
            message = self.input_value
            # Update message properties
            message.text = text
            # Preserve existing session_id from the incoming message if it exists
            existing_session_id = message.session_id
        else:
            message = Message(text=text)
            existing_session_id = None

        # Set message properties
        message.sender = self.sender
        message.sender_name = self.sender_name
        # Preserve session_id from incoming message, or use component/graph session_id
        message.session_id = (
            self.session_id or existing_session_id or (self.graph.session_id if hasattr(self, "graph") else None) or ""
        )
        message.context_id = self.context_id
        message.flow_id = self.graph.flow_id if hasattr(self, "graph") else None
        message.properties.source = self._build_source(source_id, display_name, source)

        # Store message if needed
        if message.session_id and self.should_store_message:
            stored_message = await self.send_message(message)
            self.message.value = stored_message
            message = stored_message

        self.status = message
        return message

    def _serialize_data(self, data: Data) -> str:
        """Serialize Data object to JSON string."""
        # Convert data.data to JSON-serializable format
        serializable_data = jsonable_encoder(data.data)
        # Serialize with orjson, enabling pretty printing with indentation
        json_bytes = orjson.dumps(serializable_data, option=orjson.OPT_INDENT_2)
        # Convert bytes to string and wrap in Markdown code blocks
        return "```json\n" + json_bytes.decode("utf-8") + "\n```"

    def _validate_input(self) -> None:
        """Validate the input data and raise ValueError if invalid."""
        if self.input_value is None:
            msg = "Input data cannot be None"
            raise ValueError(msg)
        if isinstance(self.input_value, list) and not all(
            isinstance(item, Message | Data | DataFrame | str) for item in self.input_value
        ):
            invalid_types = [
                type(item).__name__
                for item in self.input_value
                if not isinstance(item, Message | Data | DataFrame | str)
            ]
            msg = f"Expected Data or DataFrame or Message or str, got {invalid_types}"
            raise TypeError(msg)
        if not isinstance(
            self.input_value,
            Message | Data | DataFrame | str | list | Generator | type(None),
        ):
            type_name = type(self.input_value).__name__
            msg = f"Expected Data or DataFrame or Message or str, Generator or None, got {type_name}"
            raise TypeError(msg)

    def convert_to_string(self) -> str | Generator[Any, None, None]:
        """Convert input data to string with proper error handling."""
        self._validate_input()
        if isinstance(self.input_value, list):
            clean_data: bool = getattr(self, "clean_data", False)
            return "\n".join([safe_convert(item, clean_data=clean_data) for item in self.input_value])
        if isinstance(self.input_value, Generator):
            return self.input_value
        return safe_convert(self.input_value)

from collections.abc import Generator
from typing import Any

import orjson
from fastapi.encoders import jsonable_encoder

from lfx.base.io.chat import ChatComponent
from lfx.helpers.data import safe_convert
from lfx.inputs.inputs import BoolInput, DropdownInput, HandleInput, MessageTextInput
from lfx.schema.data import Data
from lfx.schema.dataframe import DataFrame
from lfx.schema.message import Message
from lfx.schema.properties import Source
from lfx.template.field.base import Output
from lfx.utils.constants import (
    MESSAGE_SENDER_AI,
    MESSAGE_SENDER_NAME_AI,
    MESSAGE_SENDER_USER,
)


class ChatOutput(ChatComponent):
    display_name = "Chat Output"
    description = "Display a chat message in the Playground."
    documentation: str = "https://docs.langflow.org/chat-input-and-output"
    icon = "MessagesSquare"
    name = "ChatOutput"
    minimized = True

    inputs = [
        HandleInput(
            name="input_value",
            display_name="Inputs",
            info="Message to be passed as output.",
            input_types=["Data", "DataFrame", "Message"],
            required=True,
        ),
        BoolInput(
            name="should_store_message",
            display_name="Store Messages",
            info="Store the message in the history.",
            value=True,
            advanced=True,
        ),
        DropdownInput(
            name="sender",
            display_name="Sender Type",
            options=[MESSAGE_SENDER_AI, MESSAGE_SENDER_USER],
            value=MESSAGE_SENDER_AI,
            advanced=True,
            info="Type of sender.",
        ),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="sender_name",
            display_name="Sender Name",
            info="Name of the sender.",
            value=MESSAGE_SENDER_NAME_AI,
            advanced=True,
        ),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="session_id",
            display_name="Session ID",
            info="The session ID of the chat. If empty, the current session ID parameter will be used.",
            advanced=True,
        ),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="context_id",
            display_name="Context ID",
            info="The context ID of the chat. Adds an extra layer to the local memory.",
            value="",
            advanced=True,
        ),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="data_template",
            display_name="Data Template",
            value="{text}",
            advanced=True,
            info="Template to convert Data to Text. If left empty, it will be dynamically set to the Data's text key.",
        ),
        BoolInput(
            name="clean_data",
            display_name="Basic Clean Data",
            value=True,
            advanced=True,
            info="Whether to clean data before converting to string.",
        ),
    ]
    outputs = [
        Output(
            display_name="Output Message",
            name="message",
            method="message_response",
        ),
    ]

    def _build_source(self, id_: str | None, display_name: str | None, source: str | None) -> Source:
        source_dict = {}
        if id_:
            source_dict["id"] = id_
        if display_name:
            source_dict["display_name"] = display_name
        if source:
            # Handle case where source is a ChatOpenAI object
            if hasattr(source, "model_name"):
                source_dict["source"] = source.model_name
            elif hasattr(source, "model"):
                source_dict["source"] = str(source.model)
            else:
                source_dict["source"] = str(source)
        return Source(**source_dict)

    async def message_response(self) -> Message:
        # First convert the input to string if needed
        text = self.convert_to_string()

        # Get source properties
        source, _, display_name, source_id = self.get_properties_from_source_component()

        # Create or use existing Message object
        if isinstance(self.input_value, Message) and not self.is_connected_to_chat_input():
            message = self.input_value
            # Update message properties
            message.text = text
            # Preserve existing session_id from the incoming message if it exists
            existing_session_id = message.session_id
        else:
            message = Message(text=text)
            existing_session_id = None

        # Set message properties
        message.sender = self.sender
        message.sender_name = self.sender_name
        # Preserve session_id from incoming message, or use component/graph session_id
        message.session_id = (
            self.session_id or existing_session_id or (self.graph.session_id if hasattr(self, "graph") else None) or ""
        )
        message.context_id = self.context_id
        message.flow_id = self.graph.flow_id if hasattr(self, "graph") else None
        message.properties.source = self._build_source(source_id, display_name, source)

        # Store message if needed
        if message.session_id and self.should_store_message:
            stored_message = await self.send_message(message)
            self.message.value = stored_message
            message = stored_message

        self.status = message
        return message

    def _serialize_data(self, data: Data) -> str:
        """Serialize Data object to JSON string."""
        # Convert data.data to JSON-serializable format
        serializable_data = jsonable_encoder(data.data)
        # Serialize with orjson, enabling pretty printing with indentation
        json_bytes = orjson.dumps(serializable_data, option=orjson.OPT_INDENT_2)
        # Convert bytes to string and wrap in Markdown code blocks
        return "```json\n" + json_bytes.decode("utf-8") + "\n```"

    def _validate_input(self) -> None:
        """Validate the input data and raise ValueError if invalid."""
        if self.input_value is None:
            msg = "Input data cannot be None"
            raise ValueError(msg)
        if isinstance(self.input_value, list) and not all(
            isinstance(item, Message | Data | DataFrame | str) for item in self.input_value
        ):
            invalid_types = [
                type(item).__name__
                for item in self.input_value
                if not isinstance(item, Message | Data | DataFrame | str)
            ]
            msg = f"Expected Data or DataFrame or Message or str, got {invalid_types}"
            raise TypeError(msg)
        if not isinstance(
            self.input_value,
            Message | Data | DataFrame | str | list | Generator | type(None),
        ):
            type_name = type(self.input_value).__name__
            msg = f"Expected Data or DataFrame or Message or str, Generator or None, got {type_name}"
            raise TypeError(msg)

    def convert_to_string(self) -> str | Generator[Any, None, None]:
        """Convert input data to string with proper error handling."""
        self._validate_input()
        if isinstance(self.input_value, list):
            clean_data: bool = getattr(self, "clean_data", False)
            return "\n".join([safe_convert(item, clean_data=clean_data) for item in self.input_value])
        if isinstance(self.input_value, Generator):
            return self.input_value
        return safe_convert(self.input_value)
